summary: Ukrainian forces reported drone activity across multiple fronts, while Russian sources denied a claimed Ukrainian drone strike on a Belarusian children's bus in Bryansk. Threats of Shahed-136 strike groups from Primorsko-Akhtarsk and Khalino airfields and ballistic missile launches toward Sumy and Chernihiv were noted. G7 leaders pledged increased air defence deliveries to Ukraine.
key_points: Ukrainian drones were reported operating in the Zaporozhye region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30972).

A Ukrainian drone allegedly attacked a bus with a Belarusian children's soccer team on the A240 highway near Bryansk, killing one and injuring six, but the Russian General Staff denied any Ukrainian UAV use in the area (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30977; Source: @UkraineNow/82006).
New groups of strike UAVs entered Chernihiv region from the north (Source: @kpszsu/65466).
Shahed-136 strike UAVs are threatened to launch from Primorsko-Akhtarsk (up to 8 groups) and Khalino (up to 4 groups) airfields (Source: @eRadarrua/97002).
Ballistic missile threats were reported over Sumy heading toward Chernihiv and Romny (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67833).
G7 leaders agreed to increase air defence system supplies to Ukraine (Source: @UkraineNow/81982).
Forced evacuation of families with children from 23 settlements in Dnipropetrovsk region was announced (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30978).
High‑level Ukraine‑NATO council meeting will not take place at the Ankara summit, though Zelensky will attend on the sidelines (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30981).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian drone activity continues across several fronts, including Zaporozhye and Chernihiv, while Russian authorities deny a reported Ukrainian UAV strike on a Belarusian children's bus near Bryansk. Threats of Shahed‑136 strike groups from Primorsko‑Akhtarsk and Khalino airfields and ballistic missile launches toward Sumy and Chernihiv heighten the risk of escalation. G7 leaders pledged additional air‑defence deliveries to support Ukraine.

points: Ukrainian forces claim they are conducting operations in the Zaporozhye region, describing their troops as "true professionals" (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30972).
Ukrainian drones were reported heading toward the border area between Kharkiv and Sumy regions (Source: @eRadarrua/97072).
A forced evacuation of families with children from 23 settlements in Dnipropetrovsk region was ordered, affecting nearly 3,800 children (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30978).
Ukrainian authorities announced that no UAVs were used against the Bryansk bus incident during the reported time frame (Source: @UkraineNow/82006).
Russian General Staff stated that Ukrainian UAVs did not strike targets in Bryansk region, labeling the claim an information provocation (Source: @UkraineNow/82006).
Russian sources reported a Boeing 737‑800 from Sochi returning to its departure airport due to a technical fault (Source: @tass_agency/381174).
Russian media circulated the claim of a Ukrainian drone hitting a bus with Belarusian children, which was later denied by Russian officials (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30977).
A group of hostile UAVs from Bryansk region entered northern Chernihiv, according to Ukrainian monitoring (Source: @kpszsu/65453).
Strike UAV groups entered Chernihiv region from the north, indicating increased aerial pressure on the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/65466).
Ballistic missile threats were reported over Sumy, targeting routes toward Chernihiv and Romny (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67833).
UAVs were observed heading toward Trostyanets and Boromlya in Sumy region (Source: @kpszsu/65470).
A reconnaissance UAV was spotted north of Velykyi Sorochyntsi in Poltava, moving toward Myrhorod (Source: @kpszsu/65423).
New groups of strike UAVs entered Chernihiv from the north, suggesting heightened activity along the northern front (Source: @kpszsu/65466).
A hostile UAV group from Bryansk entered northern Chernihiv (Source: @kpszsu/65453).
Shahed‑136 strike groups are prepared for launch from Primorsko‑Akhtarsk and Khalino, potentially affecting northern sectors (Source: @eRadarrua/97002).
UAV activity was reported in Kyiv region north of Pripyat and near the border with Chernihiv (Source: @eRadarrua/97036).

Ukrainian drones attacked civil infrastructure in Zaporizhzhia, causing one death and seven injuries, with damage to residential buildings, an office centre and a university (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/81983).
Strike UAVs from the Black Sea were reported heading toward Ochakiv (Source: @kpszsu/65464).
UAVs from the Black Sea were observed moving toward Odessa/Chornomorske (Source: @kpszsu/65450).
UAV groups from Kherson region were directed toward the border of Mykolaiv and Dnipropetrovsk oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/65463).
Shahed‑136 strike UAVs are threatened to launch from Primorsko‑Akhtarsk (up to 8 groups) and Khalino (up to 4 groups) airfields (Source: @eRadarrua/97002).
Multiple UAV groups were reported across Poltava, Sumy, Chernihiv, and Kharkiv regions, indicating widespread aerial operations (Source: @eRadarrua/97072; @kpszsu/65470; @kpszsu/65423).
Ballistic missile alerts were issued for Sumy, Chernihiv, and Kyiv districts, including a missile impact in Darnitskyi district of Kyiv (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67842).
Russian denial of Ukrainian UAV use in Bryansk follows earlier claims of a drone strike on a civilian bus (Source: @UkraineNow/82006).

key_developments: Ukrainian drones operating in Zaporozhye region, described as professional actions (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30972).

Claimed Ukrainian drone strike on a Belarusian children's bus near Bryansk resulted in one death and six injuries, later denied by Russian General Staff (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30977; Source: @UkraineNow/82006).
New strike UAV groups entered Chernihiv from the north, expanding aerial pressure on the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/65466).
Shahed‑136 UAV launch threats from Primorsko‑Akhtarsk (up to 8 groups) and Khalino (up to 4 groups) airfields (Source: @eRadarrua/97002).
Ballistic missile threats over Sumy heading toward Chernihiv and Romny, with alerts in Kyiv district (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67833; Source: @mon1tor_ua/67842).
G7 leaders committed to increased air defence system deliveries to Ukraine (Source: @UkraineNow/81982).
Forced evacuation of families with children from 23 settlements in Dnipropetrovsk region, affecting nearly 3,800 children (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30978).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian UAV activity is spreading across multiple axes, notably in Zaporozhye, Chernihiv, and the southern Black Sea corridor, indicating a coordinated effort to pressure Russian positions. Russian denial of the Bryansk bus incident suggests a parallel information warfare front, aiming to counteract Ukrainian claims. The preparation of Shahed‑136 strike groups from two Russian airfields and the reported ballistic missile launches toward Sumy and Kyiv raise the risk of rapid escalation, especially if missile trajectories intersect civilian areas.
sections.regional_response: Western allies, particularly G7 members, have pledged additional air defence systems, reflecting concern over the increasing UAV and missile threats. NATO's focus on Ukraine's needs at the Ankara summit, despite Zelensky's limited participation, underscores continued diplomatic support. Ukrainian authorities are conducting large‑scale evacuations in Dnipropetrovsk, indicating a precautionary response to anticipated attacks.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring should prioritize the identified UAV launch points at Primorsko‑Akhtarsk and Khalino for early warning of Shahed‑136 sorties. Ballistic missile trajectories toward Sumy, Chernihiv, and Kyiv districts require heightened radar coverage and rapid interceptor readiness. The denial of the Bryansk bus strike by Russian officials suggests potential disinformation campaigns; cross‑verification of UAV activity in border regions is essential.
